meaning: n.一阵喷烟,肿块,蓬松,吹嘘,宣传广告v.喷出,张开,夸张,(使)膨胀,(使)骄傲,喘息,鼓吹,吹捧
examples: I took a puff on the cigarette and started coughing.
The magician vanished in a puff of smoke.
I was starting to puff a little from the climb.
Pro Forma Puff Jobs 12.09.02 (Free registration required.) Investors are clamoring for straight talk about profits.
Mr. Picard makes his puff pastry from scratch, yet he said decent pre-made mille-feuille exists.
